title: alchemilla palmata subsp. pratensis
intro: Alchemilla palmata subsp. pratensis, also known as the Lady's Mantle, is a perennial plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its unique, downy leaves and delicate white or pink flowers. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, making it effective in treating various health issues such as diarrhea, bleeding, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Lady's Mantle include its ability to soothe menstrual cramps, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support women's health and alleviate symptoms associated with menopause.
